# unifi Provider

Terraform provider for the UniFi Network integration API.

This provider follows the committed UniFi OpenAPI snapshot and only exposes the parts of the current integration API that make sense for Terraform-managed configuration. Operational actions such as device adoption and controller statistics are intentionally out of scope.

## Scope

Managed resources:

- [`unifi_network`](./resources/network.md)
- [`unifi_wifi_broadcast`](./resources/wifi_broadcast.md)
- [`unifi_firewall_zone`](./resources/firewall_zone.md)
- [`unifi_firewall_policy`](./resources/firewall_policy.md)
- [`unifi_firewall_policy_ordering`](./resources/firewall_policy_ordering.md)
- [`unifi_traffic_matching_list`](./resources/traffic_matching_list.md)
- [`unifi_dns_policy`](./resources/dns_policy.md)
- [`unifi_acl_rule`](./resources/acl_rule.md)
- [`unifi_acl_rule_ordering`](./resources/acl_rule_ordering.md)

Read-only data sources:

- [`unifi_site`](./data-sources/site.md)
- [`unifi_device`](./data-sources/device.md)
- [`unifi_network`](./data-sources/network.md)
- [`unifi_wifi_broadcast`](./data-sources/wifi_broadcast.md)
- [`unifi_firewall_zone`](./data-sources/firewall_zone.md)
- [`unifi_firewall_policy`](./data-sources/firewall_policy.md)
- [`unifi_traffic_matching_list`](./data-sources/traffic_matching_list.md)
- [`unifi_dns_policy`](./data-sources/dns_policy.md)
- [`unifi_acl_rule`](./data-sources/acl_rule.md)
- [`unifi_vpn_server`](./data-sources/vpn_server.md)
- [`unifi_site_to_site_vpn_tunnel`](./data-sources/site_to_site_vpn_tunnel.md)
- [`unifi_dpi_application`](./data-sources/dpi_application.md)
- [`unifi_dpi_application_category`](./data-sources/dpi_application_category.md)
- [`unifi_country`](./data-sources/country.md)
- [`unifi_radius_profile`](./data-sources/radius_profile.md)
- [`unifi_device_tag`](./data-sources/device_tag.md)
- [`unifi_wan`](./data-sources/wan.md)
- [`unifi_switch_stack`](./data-sources/switch_stack.md)
- [`unifi_mc_lag_domain`](./data-sources/mc_lag_domain.md)
- [`unifi_lag`](./data-sources/lag.md)

## Firewall Prerequisite

Zone-based firewall must be enabled manually in the UniFi Network UI before Terraform can manage:

- [`unifi_firewall_zone`](./resources/firewall_zone.md)
- [`unifi_firewall_policy`](./resources/firewall_policy.md)
- [`unifi_firewall_policy_ordering`](./resources/firewall_policy_ordering.md)

If zone-based firewall is not enabled on the target site, the controller returns `api.firewall.zone-based-firewall-not-configured` and the firewall resources and data sources in this provider will not work for that site.

## Example Usage
```terraform
# Configure the UniFi provider with an integration API endpoint and API key.
terraform {
  required_providers {
    unifi = {
      source  = "badgerops/unifi"
      version = "0.2.7"
    }
  }
}

provider "unifi" {
  api_url        = "https://unifi.example.com"
  api_key        = "replace-me"
  allow_insecure = false
}
```

## Documentation Generation Workflow

The checked-in files under `docs/` are generated from:

- Terraform provider, resource, and data source schemas in `internal/provider`
- provider, resource, and data source examples under `examples/`
- resource import examples under `examples/resources`
- this provider template under `templates/index.md.tmpl`

Contributor workflow:

1. Update schema code and example files together.
2. Run `make docs-generate` to refresh generated markdown in `docs/`.
3. Run `make docs-check` to verify `docs/`, `templates/`, and `examples/` are in sync.
4. Commit both the source changes and the regenerated docs.

Repository enforcement:

- CI runs `make docs-check` on pull requests and on pushes to the default branch
- `.pre-commit-config.yaml` runs the same check before commits when `pre-commit` is installed

## Provider Schema

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `api_key` (String, Sensitive) API key created in the UniFi Network integrations UI.
- `api_url` (String) Base URL for the UniFi Network API. The provider appends `/integration` when it is not already present.

### Optional

- `allow_insecure` (Boolean) Disable TLS certificate verification. Only use this against trusted development systems.
